rationale: The synthesized postcondition is `Premise ==> Equal(r1,r2)` where Equal requires r1.0==r2.0 AND r1.1==r2.1 unconditionally, but Premise only constrains r1.1 (resp. r2.1) when r1.0 (resp. r2.0) is false. When r1.0==r2.0==true the Premise is consistent with r1.1==true and r2.1==false (e.g., set via the g_r1_1_is_true / g_r2_1_is_false assumes), so Premise && !Equal is satisfiable — the implication is not a tautology. The 'r1.0==r2.0' part and the 'both false ==> r1.1==r2.1' part both verify individually (asserts 1 and 2 succeed); only the full implication fails. No proof annotation can close this without an axiom (assume/admit/external_body), which are forbidden. Attempts: (1) single assert of the full implication — assertion failed; (2) empty proof block — postcondition not satisfied; (3) decomposed lets + helper asserts + full implication — helpers pass, full implication still fails.
